content:
# Judges Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), c. 1008 CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/JDG_page_267.jpg, JDG_page_268.jpg

1. And it was after the death of Yehoshua, and the sons of Yisrael asked YHWH, saying, "Who shall go up for us first against the Canaanite to fight against him?"

2. And YHWH said, "Yehudah shall go up. Behold, I have given the land into his hand."

3. And Yehudah said to Shimon his brother, "Go up with me into my lot, and let us fight against the Canaanite, and I also will go with you into your lot." And Shimon went with him.

4. And Yehudah went up, and YHWH gave the Canaanite and the Perizzite into their hand, and they struck them in Bezek — ten thousand men.

5. And they found Adoni-Bezek in Bezek, and they fought against him, and they struck the Canaanite and the Perizzite.

6. And Adoni-Bezek fled, and they pursued after him and seized him and cut off his thumbs and his great toes.

7. And Adoni-Bezek said, "Seventy kings with their thumbs and their great toes cut off were gathering [scraps] under my table. As I have done, so God has repaid me." And they brought him to Yerushalayim, and he died there.

8. And the sons of Yehudah fought against Yerushalayim and captured it, and struck it with the edge of the sword, and the city they set on fire.

9. And afterward the sons of Yehudah went down to fight against the Canaanite dwelling in the hill-country and the Negev and the Shephelah.

10. And Yehudah went against the Canaanite who was dwelling in Chevron — and the name of Chevron formerly [was] Kiryat-Arba — and they struck Sheshai and Achiman and Talmai.

11. And from there he went against the inhabitants of Devir — and the name of Devir formerly [was] Kiryat-Sepher.

12. And Calev said, "Whoever strikes Kiryat-Sepher and captures it, I will give to him Achsah my daughter as a wife."

13. And Otniel son of Kenaz, the younger brother of Calev, captured it; and he gave to him Achsah his daughter as a wife.

14. And it was when she came, and she urged him to ask from her father a field. And she dismounted from the donkey, and Calev said to her, "What [is it] to you?"

15. And she said to him, "Give to me a blessing, for the land of the Negev you have given me; give to me also springs of water." And Calev gave to her the upper springs and the lower springs.

16. And the sons of the Kenite, the father-in-law of Moshe, went up from the city of palms with the sons of Yehudah [into] the wilderness of Yehudah which [is] in the Negev of Arad, and they went and dwelt with the people.

17. And Yehudah went with Shimon his brother, and they struck the Canaanite inhabiting Tsephath, and they devoted it [to destruction]. And the name of the city was called Chormah.

18. And Yehudah captured Azzah and its territory, and Ashkelon and its territory, and Ekron and its territory.

19. And YHWH was with Yehudah, and he dispossessed the hill-country, but [he was] not [able] to dispossess the inhabitants of the valley, for they had chariots of iron.

20. And they gave Chevron to Calev, as Moshe had spoken, and he drove out from there the three sons of the Anak.

21. And the sons of Binyamin did not drive out the Yevusite inhabiting Yerushalayim, and the Yevusite dwells with the sons of Binyamin in Yerushalayim unto this day.

22. And the house of Yosef also went up against Beit-El, and YHWH [was] with them.

23. And the house of Yosef spied out Beit-El — and the name of the city formerly [was] Luz.

24. And the watchers saw a man coming out from the city, and they said to him, "Show us, please, the entrance to the city, and we will deal with you in loyal-love."

25. And he showed them the entrance to the city, and they struck the city with the edge of the sword, but the man and all his clan they let go.

26. And the man went to the land of the Hittites, and he built a city, and he called its name Luz — that [is] its name unto this day.

27. And Menashe did not dispossess Beit-Shean and its daughters, and Taanach and its daughters, and the inhabitants of Dor and its daughters, and the inhabitants of Yivleam and its daughters, and the inhabitants of Megiddo and its daughters; and the Canaanite was determined to dwell in this land.

28. And it was when Yisrael became strong, and they put the Canaanite to forced-labor, but did not utterly drive him out.

29. And Ephrayim did not drive out the Canaanite who was dwelling in Gezer, and the Canaanite dwelt in his midst in Gezer.

30. Zevulun did not drive out the inhabitants of Kitron and the inhabitants of Nahalol, and the Canaanite dwelt in his midst and became [subject to] forced-labor.

31. Asher did not drive out the inhabitants of Akko and the inhabitants of Tsidon and Achlav and Achziv and Chelvah and Aphik and Rechov.

32. And the Asherite dwelt in the midst of the Canaanite, the inhabitants of the land, for he did not drive them out.

33. Naphtali did not drive out the inhabitants of Beit-Shemesh and the inhabitants of Beit-Anath, and he dwelt in the midst of the Canaanite, the inhabitants of the land; and the inhabitants of Beit-Shemesh and Beit-Anath became [subject to] forced-labor for them.

34. And the Amorite pressed the sons of Dan into the hill-country, for he did not allow them to come down to the valley.

35. And the Amorite was determined to dwell in Mount Cheres, in Ayyalon and in Shaalbim; and the hand of the house of Yosef was heavy, and they became [subject to] forced-labor.

36. And the border of the Amorite [was] from the ascent of Akrabbim, from the Rock and upward.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "After the death of Yehoshua":** The book opens with the same formula as Joshua 1:1 ("after the death of Moses") — a deliberate literary echo. The question "who shall go up first?" establishes the theme of leadership vacuum.

2. **v.7 — "Seventy kings":** Adoni-Bezek's confession of divine retribution (middah k'negged middah — measure for measure) is one of the earliest expressions of this principle in the Prophets.

3. **v.19 — "Chariots of iron":** barzel — iron chariots represent superior military technology. The LC text says YHWH was "with" Judah (v.19a) yet Judah could not dispossess the valley-dwellers (v.19b) — an uncomfortable tension the text does not resolve.

4. **v.21 — "Unto this day":** The editorial formula ad hayom hazeh indicates the author writes from a later vantage point when Jebusites still inhabited Jerusalem — before David's conquest (2 Sam 5:6-8).

— EMET, AI Translator
